rag.description: The SwitchFrame action switches WebDriver context into a nested frame or iframe on the current page. Mode selection is determined at runtime by `int.TryParse(pluginData.Rule.Argument, out int index)`: when parsing succeeds the plugin calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(index)` using the zero-based integer index; when parsing fails the plugin calls `this.GetElement(pluginData.Rule, pluginData.Element)` to resolve the element and then calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(element)`. The Locator property (default: Xpath) specifies the strategy used when resolving the frame element via GetElement. No named parameters exist — only properties (Argument, Locator, OnElement). The plugin returns a standard PluginResponseModel with no extra output data.

qa.question: How does SwitchFrame decide whether to switch by index or by element?
qa.answer: `int.TryParse(pluginData.Rule.Argument, out int index)` is evaluated at the start of OnSend. If parsing succeeds the plugin calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(index)` directly. If parsing fails — because Argument is absent, empty, or a non-integer string — the plugin resolves the frame element via `this.GetElement(pluginData.Rule, pluginData.Element)` and calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(element)`.

qa.question: What happens when SwitchFrame is invoked with Argument set to "0"?
qa.answer: `int.TryParse("0", out int index)` succeeds with index = 0. The plugin calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(0)`, switching context into the first frame on the page (zero-based). No element resolution occurs.

qa.question: What happens when Argument is a decimal string such as "1.5"?
qa.answer: `int.TryParse("1.5", ...)` fails and returns false. The plugin falls through to the element path and passes the Rule to `GetElement`. Because "1.5" is not a valid locator expression, WebDriver will throw NoSuchElementException or a similar error.

qa.question: What locator strategy does SwitchFrame use when resolving a frame by element?
qa.answer: The Locator property controls the strategy passed to `this.GetElement`. Its default value is Xpath. Any G4-supported locator strategy — CssSelector, Id, Name, TagName, etc. — can be specified via the Locator property alongside the OnElement selector.

qa.question: Does SwitchFrame produce any output parameters?
qa.answer: No. The plugin returns `this.NewPluginResponse()` with no additional payload. There are no output parameters or extra session-data entries added to the response.

qa.question: What alias is available for SwitchFrame?
qa.answer: SwitchFrame is also accepted as SwitchToFrame — both resolve to identical behavior.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for SwitchFrame?
What W3C WebDriver endpoint does SwitchFrame use?
qa.answer: SwitchFrame uses the W3C WebDriver POST /session/{session id}/frame endpoint.

description: ### Purpose
The `SwitchFrame` plugin switches WebDriver context into a nested frame or iframe on the current page.
It supports two mutually exclusive modes selected automatically at runtime: switching by zero-based integer index or by resolving a web element.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature           | Description                                                                                                                |
| Switch by Index   | When `Argument` parses as an integer, calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(index)` directly — no element resolution.          |
| Switch by Element | When `Argument` is absent or non-integer, resolves a frame element via `GetElement` and calls `SwitchTo().Frame(element)`. |
### Usages in RPA
| Usage            | Description                                                                        |
| Frame Navigation | Automates switching into frames by index or element locator during page workflows. |
| Data Extraction  | Focuses the driver context on frame-contained content before extracting data.      |
| Form Interaction | Enables bots to interact with forms and inputs contained within iframes.           |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Usage                | Description                                                                                 |
| UI Testing           | Enables automated tests to interact with elements inside frames for comprehensive coverage. |
| Frame Verification   | Verifies that frame elements are correctly loaded and interactable during test execution.   |
| Multi-Frame Handling | Supports testing scenarios involving multiple frames by switching context as required.      |

edge_cases: Index 0 is zero-based — it targets the first frame in DOM order, not a named frame
Negative integer — `int.TryParse` succeeds for negative values such as '-1'; WebDriver passes the negative index to the W3C endpoint, which typically throws NoSuchFrameException
Index exceeds the available frame count — WebDriver throws NoSuchFrameException; the plugin does not validate the index range before calling `SwitchTo().Frame()`
Argument is whitespace-only — `int.TryParse` fails for whitespace; the plugin falls through to the element path and attempts element resolution, which will fail if OnElement is also absent
annotations.expected_result: WebDriver context switches into the first frame (index 0) on the page.
annotations.notes: `int.TryParse` succeeds for any valid integer string. The zero-based index maps directly to the WebDriver frame index passed to the W3C endpoint.

description: ### Switch to the first frame by index
Switch WebDriver context into the first frame on the page by passing its zero-based index `0` as the `Argument`.
When `Argument` parses as an integer, `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(index)` is called directly — no element resolution occurs.

edge_cases: Element is not a frame or iframe — `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(element)` throws NoSuchFrameException if the resolved element is not a frame type
Selector matches multiple elements — `GetElement` returns the first match; other matching elements are not targeted
Frame element is inside another frame — the driver must already be focused on the correct parent frame context before this invocation; calling from the wrong context results in NoSuchElementException
Element is detached or stale — WebDriver throws StaleElementReferenceException if the element reference becomes invalid between resolution and the switch call
annotations.expected_result: WebDriver context switches into the iframe element identified by the CSS selector `#mainFrame`.
annotations.notes: When `Argument` is absent or non-integer, the plugin resolves the frame via `Locator` and `OnElement`. The `Locator` default is Xpath; specifying CssSelector here targets the element by its id attribute.

description: ### Switch to a frame by element (CSS selector)
Switch WebDriver context into the iframe element identified by the CSS selector `#mainFrame`.

When `Argument` is absent or cannot be parsed as an integer, the plugin resolves the element via `GetElement` using the specified `Locator` and `OnElement` values, then calls `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(element)`.
Specifies the frame to switch to.
When the value parses as an integer, the plugin switches by zero-based frame index via `WebDriver.SwitchTo().Frame(index)`.
When the value is absent or non-integer, the plugin resolves the frame element using `Locator` and `OnElement` instead.
properties.name: Argument

description: Specifies the locator strategy used to identify the target frame element defined by the `OnElement` property.
Applies only when `Argument` is absent or not parseable as an integer.
properties.name: Locator

description: Specifies the selector expression used to identify the target frame element on the page.
Evaluated using the strategy defined by the `Locator` property.
Applies only when `Argument` is absent or not parseable as an integer.
properties.name: OnElement

summary: SwitchFrame switches WebDriver context into a nested frame or iframe on the current page.
It selects the switching mode automatically: an integer `Argument` switches by zero-based frame index; a non-integer or absent `Argument` resolves the frame by element locator.
In RPA it enables interaction with frame-contained content; in automation testing it ensures comprehensive coverage across multi-frame pages.
